subject: Human Resources
title: Unconscious Bias in HR
description: A quick, practical guide to Unconscious Bias in HR.

hook.text: Identical resumes with different names: 'Emily' gets 50% more callbacks than 'Lakisha.' Same qualifications. Different name.

buildup.text: Unconscious biases are mental shortcuts influencing decisions without awareness—affecting hiring, promotions, and evaluations.

discovery.text: Common biases include affinity bias (liking similar people), halo effect (one trait colors all judgments), and confirmation bias.

twist.text: Awareness training alone doesn't reduce bias—studies show it can even backfire by making people feel they've 'solved' the problem.

climax.text: Systemic fixes work: structured interviews, blind resume reviews, diverse panels, and data-driven decisions reduce bias.

punchline.text: You can't think your way out of bias. You have to design your way out.

quiz.question: What is most effective at reducing unconscious bias in HR?
options: Systemic solutions like structured interviews and blind reviews
